title: Enterovirus D68 3C protease large scale purification protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f7euh","text":"This protocol details the expression and purification of enterovirus D68 3C protease construct bearing a C-terminal His-tag at large scale (\u003e6L)\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e7vro","text":"Construct / plasmid resource-name: Enterovirus D68 3C protease construct bearing a C-terminal His-tag that crystallised in the space group P212121","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":35},{"style":"sub","offset":141,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":143,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":145,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ssr9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qrh1","text":"Brief description of the plasmid: This protein yielded highly reproducible crystals upon microseeding, which typically diffracted to 1.7 Å resolution. steps: Transform Enterovirus D68 3C Protease addgene Catalog #204817 into BL21(DE3) and store a glycerol stock of this at -80 °C The D68EV3C construct encodes the 3C protease with a non-cleavable C-terminal his tag on a kanamycin resistant plasmid backbone with a T7 promoter.
Perform IMAC to extract target protein from the lysed cell mixture
Add 10 CV of wash buffer 1 to the column. Replace cap on the column and resuspend resin. Incubate at 4 °C while gently rotating for 30mins. This is to remove any DNA contaminants bound to the resin.

Using the AKTA Pure system: Sample injected onto 50mL Superloop (Cytiva) through a 0.2uM syringe filter to further remove any aggregates in the sample. Run the sample down XK 50/100 Superdex 200 pg gel filtration column at 6mL/min in gel filtration buffer, collecting 35mL aliquots in 50mL falcon tubes. Collection started after 0.4CV into elution, as the fraction collector can only hold 6x6 falcon tubes. Fraction collection parameters can be adjusted to individual FPLC system specs. The column should be pre-equilibrated in SEC buffer.

Aliquot into appropriate volumes for future usage to minimise freeze/thaw cycles. Flash-freeze in liquid nitrogen, and store at -80 °C until required. For example: The final yield from processing 243 g of cell pellet was 543 mg of pure D68 EV 3C protease
Lyse cell pellet
Lyse cells by sonication 00:00:04, On 00:00:12, Off for a total 'on' time of 00:07:00 at 50% amplitude to fully rupture the cells. Ensure pellet is °C during sonication to prevent overheating.
Resuspend the equilibrated resin with some binding buffer and add to the supernatant bottle. Incubate the resin with the supernatant for 00:30:00 while rotating or otherwise mixing gently at 4 °C

Load the resin/supernatant mix back onto the gravity flow column, retaining the flow through separately for SDS-PAGE analysis. For SDS-PAGE samples, mix 15uL sample with 5uL 4x sample buffer, supplemented with 10mM DTT.

Large scale expression of EV-D68 3C protease using single use column reactors. Setup and Operation of Single Use (SUB) Bubble Column Reactors (BCR) for Recombinant Proteins: Litre-Scale Expression of enterovirus 3C protease for Structural Biology and Drug Design (SBDD) by Mary-Ann Xavier
CV - column volume, total volume of resin in a column IMAC - immobilised metal affinity chromatography

Run SDS-PAGE of all samples from total lysis supernatant to final elution. Stain gel with Coomasssie Blue and determine which fractions contain the target protein by finding the band corresponding to the target molecular weight, 21.3 kDa. The target protein is expected to be present mostly in the elution samples, although small amounts may be found in the flow through and washes. If that is not the case, then further troubleshooting is required. SDS-PAGE analysis of IMAC fractions. The thick protein band observed in all three elutions agree with the calculated molecular weight of D68EV3C protease, 21.3 kDa.
Purify sample further by size exclusion chromatography.

Dispense 30 mL Nickle affinity resin (Ni Sepharose 6 FF, Cytiva) into a gravity flow column. Wash the resin first with ~ 20 CV distilled water to remove the storage solution and then ~ 20 CV binding buffer to equilibrate
Centrifuge the lysed cells 38000 x g, 4 °C, 1 hour to remove insoluble cell debris, and collect the supernatant in a bottle 4 °C

See Materials tab for buffer compositions. In the below examples 244 g of cell pellet was processed D68EV3C construct protein properties MW = 21.283 kDa Extinction coefficient (assume all Cys reduced)=10430 mM-1cm-1 pI = 7.21 Values determined using Expasy ProtParam Thaw and resuspend the pellet in ~8mL of lysis buffer per g of pellet. Stir gently with magnetic stir bar at Room temperature for 00:30:00 to allow lysozyme and bezonase to start breaking down cell components.
Pool and dilute the elutions with base buffer to reduce the sample imidazole concentration to 100 millimolar (mM) .

Remove any solid aggregates from the sample by centrifugation at 20000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes , then immediately draw up the supernatant with a 50mL syringe and a blunt-tip fill needle, taking care not to disturb the pellet. This is to remove as much solid particles from the injection sample as possible, so as to not clog the in-line filter or frit of the column.

Run the peak SEC fractions on SDS PAGE to assess purity. For example: Below two SEC runs were carried out due to large volume of sample. Chromatogram of SEC run 1: fractions 3B3, 4A1 and 4A2 were analysed by SDS-PAGE. Chromatogram of SEC run 2: fractions 3B2, 3B3, 4A1 and 4A2 were analysed by SDS-PAGE SDS-PAGE analysis of SEC fractions: Samples from both the above SEC runs are shown, band observed at 21.3 kDa agrees with the calculated molecular weight of D68EV3C protease.

Concentrate the diluted sample with Vivaspin 50 (10kDa MWCO) tangential flow concentrators connected to a peristaltic pump, to a final volume of under 30 mL . Tangential flow concentrators should be placed on ice to keep the sample cool. Peristatic pumps generate heat during operation which may denature the target protein. If the final concentration resulted in sample more than 30mL, gel filtration may need to be carried out in multiple batches.

Take the fractions that contain the cleanest target protein and concentrate to 21 mg/mL using a 10 kDa MWCO centrifugal concentrator. Take 1 µL of the final sample for SDS-PAGE, and another for mass spectroscopy. SDS-PAGE of the final purified D68EV3CPROA. Intact Mass-spectroscopy of purified D68EV3C protease sample. Mass spec result showing the purified D68EV3C has the expected molecular weight, 21.283 kDa

09/01/2025 Corrected benzonase wash step Added final sample SDS-PAGE image 10/02/2025 Large Scale expression protocol added Updated Addgene ID as reagent Updated author affiliations Updated safety warnings Updated grant ID

Drain wash buffer 1, then wash the resin with 10 CV of wash buffer 2. Allow wash buffer to pass through completely. This is to remove non-specific, weak binding of contaminant proteins from the resin for a cleaner elution. Collect washes separately for SDS-PAGE analysis.
Elute the protein with 1 CV of elution buffer.

Repeat step 6.6 a further 2 times, collecting a total of 3 separate elution fractions. This is to ensure maximum retrieval of protein from the resin. Measured the A280 values of the elution fractions to estimate the protein content For example: E1: A280=1.16 E2: A280=6.22 E3: A280=3.24
creator: korvus.wang
